t_ch: 换言之，我们还要在匹诺康尼停留一段时间。
t_en: In other words, we won't be leaving Penacony anytime soon.

t_ch: 或者也可以回前几站看看，检查下星图和航路有没有出错，不知道泰科铵大球馆的洞填上了没。
t_en: Or we could also revisit our previous stops to make sure that the astral charts and navigation routes are all functioning fine? I wonder if the hole in the Taikiyan Stadium has been repaired.

t_ch: 离开空间站后，列车一路风尘仆仆，几乎没有停下来整顿的时间。就把这阵子当作假期的尾声，好好利用吧。
t_en: The Express has been on the go almost the whole time since we left the space station. We've barely had any time to catch our breath. Let's take this as an opportunity to wrap up our vacation instead, and make the most out of this break.

t_ch: 数周后 匹诺康尼 黄金的时刻
t_en: Several weeks later, Golden Hour, Penacony

t_ch: 但要我说，制造混乱也是一种维稳的手段。
t_en: Creating chaos is also a means to maintaining social stability, if you ask me.

t_ch: 有功夫闲聊，不如先干活吧。
t_en: If you have the time for mindless gossip, you're probably better off spending it on getting actual work done.

t_ch: 说是持续调查，但哪用得着啊，危险早都过去了，现在也只是做做样子。
t_en: They say this is all part of "ongoing investigations," but is there even any point to doing this? The threat's long been neutralized. They're just putting on a show.

t_ch: 就像你和我，家系和家系的合作突然频繁起来，不都是做给外人看的？
t_en: I mean, look at us, and this sudden surge in inter-Family cooperative efforts. It's nothing but a pretense for those outsiders, isn't it?

t_ch: 看来你知道不少内幕啊，那我倒好奇了……
t_en: Sounds like you've got insider intel. Now I'm curious...

t_ch: 所以消息是真是假？前阵子的动静真是橡木家系出了问题？
t_en: Are the stories going around true? Was the furor that broke out a while ago really a result of trouble within the Oak Family?

t_ch: 这可难为我了，刚才不是说了吗？混乱也是一种维稳的手段。
t_en: Those are some difficult questions... Didn't I just say that creating chaos is also a means to maintaining social stability?

t_ch: 也不知是谁在推波助澜，外头同时传着十几种谣言，一条比一条离谱。
t_en: Who knows who's been fanning the flames, but there are a dozen rumors going around out there, each one more outlandish than the last.

t_ch: 嗯，我也听过，像什么「公司策划了一切」的阴谋论，还有什么…混乱是被一场车祸终结的，乱七八糟。所以真相是什么？
t_en: Oh, I've heard those. Conspiracy theories about how the IPC had orchestrated everything and... What was it again? That the chaos was only quelled because of an accident? Ugh, it makes no sense. So, what's the lowdown?

t_ch: 真相恐怕早就被流言掩埋了。唯一能确定的，就是有人担了责任。
t_en: The truth has probably been long buried under all the hearsay. All I can say for sure is that someone's taken the rap for it.

t_ch: 你是说…前橡木家主？
t_en: You mean... the former Oak Family head?

t_ch: 对，谐乐大典意外中断，他一个人背了大半锅。在那之后就没露过面。
t_en: Bingo! Most of the blame was pinned on him when the Charmony Festival was abruptly canceled. Since then, not a single soul's seen him.

t_ch: 别说去向，连生死都没人讲得清，多吓人。说不定…就像曾经的黑布林家系一样，我们不知不觉见证了历史啊。
t_en: Heck, forget his whereabouts — nobody even knows if he's still alive! Talk about scary. For all we know, we might have watched something go down in history — like what happened to the Blackplum Family back then — without even realizing it.

t_ch: 可关于那位家主，我还知道另一种说法……
t_en: I've heard another theory about that family head, though...

t_ch: 这位客人，实在抱歉。筑梦师正在排查邻近路段，相关设施都停用了。麻烦您暂时绕行。
t_en: I beg your pardon, sirs. Dreamweavers are carrying out inspections on the routes around here, and all affected facilities have been closed off. Kindly take a detour in the meantime.

t_ch: 怎么回事，是星核的影响？匹诺康尼还能让人安心做梦么？
t_en: What happened? Is this because of the Stellaron? Geez, are we Penaconians never gonna dream in peace again?

t_ch: 请放心。星核早就被封印了，这会儿只是在做例行排查，您也可以理解为加强安保。
t_en: There is no cause for alarm, sir. The Stellaron has already been sealed. This is just a routine inspection. A move to heighten security, if you will.

t_ch: 守护每一位宾客的美梦，家族会为此竭尽全力。
t_en: Please rest assured that The Family will do everything in our power to protect the sweet dreams of every guest.

t_ch: 原来如此，打扰二位了。
t_en: I see. We'll leave the two of you to your work, then.
...Hmm?

t_ch: 抱歉，这位客人——
t_en: Excuse me, sir...

t_ch: 我们是不是在哪见过？
t_en: Have we met before?

t_ch: 是吗……
何时何地呢？
t_en: Do you recall when or where we might've met?

t_ch: 或许是我记错了，失礼了，请二位继续享受美梦吧。
t_en: My bad, I must be mistaken. Sorry about that. Sweet dreams to both of you, sirs.

t_ch: 你可真会惹是生非。那么多条路，偏偏往猎犬眼皮子底下钻。
t_en: You sure have a knack for stirring up trouble. Of all the routes available, you had to take the one that Bloodhound's keeping watch over.

t_ch: 有你在，多半不会有问题。退一步说，即便「同谐」的伪装被人识破，我们也有办法逃离。
t_en: We shouldn't run into any problems with you around. Even if they see through the Harmony's power of disguise, there'll be a way for us to escape.

t_ch: 多少替人考虑下啊，匹诺康尼的头号逃犯。光是和你站在一起，就够惹祸上身的了。
t_en: Spare a thought for me, will you? Just being in your company is plenty enough to get me into trouble, Mr. Prime Fugitive of Penacony.

t_ch: 这也是我必须与你同行的理由。眼下，你是我在匹诺康尼唯一能仰赖的人。
t_en: And that is exactly why I need you with me. You're the only one I can rely on in Penacony now.

t_ch: 哼，唯一能仰赖的人……
t_en: Hmph, the only one you can rely on, huh.

t_ch: 那她呢？
t_en: What about her?

t_ch: 至少她没有受到我的牵连。
t_en: At least she was spared from the consequences of my actions.

t_ch: 怎么，觉得很失望？
t_en: What a shame, huh?

t_ch: 为什么这么说？
t_en: Why do you say that?

t_ch: 只是觉得比起安慰，你可能更需要打击。
t_en: Just thought that being mean might be more effective than consoling you.

t_ch: 像你这样的人我见过太多。经历一场失败，就巴不得所有人都来数落一番，只有这样心里才能踏实。好像有人批评，犯的错就有机会弥补。
t_en: I've seen plenty like you. Eager for everyone to take you to task after a setback because that's the only way you'll feel better. As though the more they condemn you, the more likely you can rectify your mistake.

t_ch: 但如果我真想伤害你，肯定会换另一种方式。比如…同情？
t_en: That said, if I'd really wanted to hit you where it hurts, I'd use a different approach... Like a show of sympathy, for example.

t_ch: 你说得对，我无法反驳。
t_en: You're right. I won't deny that.

t_ch: 她这会儿还在匹诺康尼吧，不去见见？
t_en: She should still be in Penacony. Aren't you going to look for her?

t_ch: 不必了。既然你如此热衷于揣测我内心的想法，那应该不难明白……
t_en: That won't be necessary. I'm sure you already know why, considering your vested interest in reading my thoughts.

t_ch: 以「逃犯」的身份与她相见，我做不到。
t_en: ...I can't bring myself to see her when I'm a fugitive.

t_ch: 两名猎犬。和之前的二人不同，他们看上去有要务在身。
t_en: I see two Bloodhounds... They appear to be on official business, unlike the pair from earlier.

t_ch: 艾迪恩公园出什么事了？
t_en: What's happening at Aideen Park?

t_ch: 喂——你就光顾着自己看了？
t_en: Hey! Heeeey! In case you didn't notice, I can't see anything!

t_ch: 抱歉。我忘了你长得不高。
t_en: My apologies. I forgot that you're not very tall.

t_ch: 真搞不明白，为什么我非得陪你走一遭。
t_en: Remind me... Why do I have to make this trip with you again?

t_ch: 我说过，除你以外我别无选择。
t_en: We've been through this. You're the only one I can turn to now.

t_ch: 况且，你我二人曾是水火不容的关系，让你受到牵连，不会有悖我的良心。
t_en: Besides, we once lived a cat-and-dog existence. Embroiling you in my affairs won't weigh on my conscience.

t_ch: 这话都讲得出，你确实没什么良心。
t_en: You certainly don't have much of one if you can bring yourself to say something like that.

t_ch: 但即便如此，你也会偿还我的人情。毕竟我之于你，恩同再造。
t_en: Yet you will repay this favor all the same. After all, you're only alive today because of me. You owe me that much.

t_ch: 我了个…哪有人这么自夸的？
t_en: ...Holy moly! Can you get any more conceited?

t_ch: 你被丢弃在荒野，行将就木的时候，是我为你指明生路，带你逃离死地。
t_en: When you were abandoned in the wild and near your end, I showed you a way to survive and helped you escape the jaws of death.

t_ch: 你也曾灵光尽失，不知前途，同样是我竭尽手段，保住了你在家族中的位置。
t_en: When you were at your wits' end and felt lost about your future, I did everything within my power to help you safeguard your position in The Family.

t_ch: 行了行了，说起话来一套一套的。可还人情是一码事，送死是另一码。
t_en: Okay, okay. I get it already. You have a way with words, I'll give you that. Still, it's one thing to repay a favor, but quite another to knowingly put myself in harm's way.

t_ch: 我是真不明白，为什么非得回匹诺康尼？公司给你派任务了？
t_en: I don't understand. Why are you hell-bent on going back to Penacony? Is this an IPC mission?

t_ch: 「哦，你可以重获自由，但作为代价，必须与过去的家人为敌」——类似这样？
t_en: Did they make you a deal? "You can have your freedom back, but in return, you must turn against all whom you once called family" or something like that?

t_ch: 很遗憾，那位慈玉女士没有给出任何指示。
t_en: Regrettably, Lady Bonajade didn't drop so much as a hint about it.

t_ch: 走吧，你自由了，妄图超越本分的神选者。把自己的羽翼折断，到人间去，走在大地上，看看这人世真正的模样。
t_en: Go now. You are free — O chosen one, who dared to exceed his bounds. Sever your wings, descend to the mortal realm, and walk their lands. See what this world is truly like.

t_ch: 我不会接受你们的施舍。
t_en: I will not accept your charity.

t_ch: 我说过，这是一份交易，而且不需要你立刻回应。收获不在一朝一夕，我最擅长的事情就是等待。美梦还在继续，夜晚还长，你有的是时间慢慢考虑。
t_en: As I mentioned earlier: It's a trade, and you don't have to give me an answer right now. Rewards are not reaped in a day, and if there's one thing I'm best at, it's waiting. The sweet dream still continues, and the night is still long — you have plenty of time to contemplate your answer.

t_ch: 她只留下意味深长的话语，便将自由交还给我。至于我能否偿付自由的代价……
t_en: She only left me with a most intriguing message before setting me free. As for whether I can afford the price of my freedom...

t_ch: 至少我得先知道代价为何物。
t_en: I'll only know when I find out what it is.

t_ch: 欠公司人情的多半没好下场。
t_en: But nothing good ever comes out of being in the IPC's debt.

t_ch: 但那也是以后的事了。凡清算皆需时日，如今，先让我行使这自由的权利吧。
t_en: I'll cross that bridge when I get to it. For now, there's still some time before that payment is due, so why not exercise the right to freedom that I've just regained?

t_ch: 用来自投罗网？
t_en: By walking straight into a trap?

t_ch: 或是偿还旧事。
t_en: Or by making amends for past deeds.

t_ch: 此去一别，便是永别。离开匹诺康尼前，我想再做一次回望。
t_en: When it's time to go, this will be farewell for good. So, before I leave Penacony, I'd like to take some time to look back on all the events that have taken place.

t_ch: 那你远远望一眼不就得了。
t_en: Can't you just look back on it from a distance?

t_ch: 此行也有别的目的。尽管「哲学的胎儿」已经陨落，但匹诺康尼的「秩序」并非一日筑成，这美梦中恐怕还残留着橡木家系的影响。
t_en: There's something else I want to do, too. The Embryo of Philosophy may have fallen, but the Order in Penacony wasn't built in a day. This sweet dream is likely to still contain residual traces of the Oak Family's influence.

t_ch: 用不着这样美化自己，其余四大家系一样能解决问题。
t_en: Get off your high horse. The other four Families are more than capable of working things out on their own.

t_ch: 我知我罪，不应由他人为我的错误善后。
t_en: I might have gone astray, but others shouldn't have to clean up after my mistakes.

t_ch: 那你也该知道，公司不会救你第二遍。要是再落到家族手里，你就彻底完了。
t_en: Then you should also know that the IPC isn't going to help you a second time. If you fall into The Family's hands again, you're done for.

t_ch: 总有些旧习难以改变。
t_en: You know how it is — old habits die hard.

t_ch: 领带应在正中线上，衬衣不得从马甲中露出，裤线必须笔直且对齐鞋头的朝向…人在出门前就该确保一切井然有序，绝不偏移。
t_en: The tie should be on the center line, the shirt must not protrude from the vest, trouser creases should be perfectly straight, and always aligned with the tips of your shoes.... We should always ensure that everything is in order before walking out the door.

t_ch: 而在辞别故乡时，也应当如此。
t_en: The same applies when bidding my home farewell, too.

t_ch: 成，那咱们继续走吧，帮一位逃犯重返故地，真棒。
t_en: Alright, then let's keep going. Helping a fugitive return to their home... So exciting.

t_ch: 抱歉，公园设施出了些故障，正在重新布设。请之后再来吧。
t_en: Sorry, but park facilities are out of order. Refurbishment works are currently underway. Please come back again later.

t_ch: 很相似的说辞。
t_en: That sounds familiar.

t_ch: 哦，情况我大概了解，不用再汇报一遍了。
t_en: Ahem. I'm aware of the situation. You can skip the report.

t_ch: 这位先生，您在说什么呢，我……
t_en: Huh? What are you talking about? I—

t_ch: …啊，万维克先生？我真是累迷糊了，竟然没认出您。
t_en: ...Mr. Wonweek? Please excuse me. I should've recognized you earlier. I must be more tired than I realized.

t_ch: 我想了解下里头的状况，你应该不会为难吧？
t_en: I wanna know what's going on in there. You'll tell me, won't you?

t_ch: 唉，也没什么好说的，猎犬自己都没查明白。大概是星核事件的余波吧。
t_en: There's nothing much to say, actually. We Bloodhounds are just as baffled as you are. My guess is that it's simply part of the aftermath of the Stellaron incident.

t_ch: 余波？
t_en: Aftermath?

t_ch: 这位是？
t_en: You are...?

t_ch: 算是我的部下，著名的加班狂人「工作日先生」。你不认识？
t_en: Renowned workaholic, Mr. Workday. He works for me. You don't know him?

t_ch: …原来如此，失敬。
t_en: Ah, I see. Pardon my ignorance, sir.

t_ch: 说回公园，总而言之，附近出现了一些「绝对不能触犯的事」。
t_en: Coming back to the topic of the park... Let's just say that it has been recently observed that certain behaviors are not tolerated around here.

t_ch: 触犯了会怎样？
t_en: What happens if you violate the prohibitions?

t_ch: 很不可思议，会被纠正。用言语难以描述，您要亲自看看吗？
t_en: It's baffling, but corrective measures will be taken. It's kind of difficult to explain... Maybe you'd like to see for yourself?

t_ch: 行啊。那麻烦你看好入口，咱俩进去瞧瞧吧。
t_en: Yeah, that works. Can you keep watch over the entrance? We'll go in and have a look for ourselves.

t_ch: 请带上这份笔记吧。希望能帮上你们。
t_en: Please take these notes with you. They may come in handy.

t_ch: 你不必说那句话的，会显得我们十分可疑。
t_en: You shouldn't have said that just now. It'll only make us look suspicious.

t_ch: 哪句？
t_en: Said what?

t_ch: 「工作日先生」。
t_en: "Mr. Workday."

t_ch: 哼，陈述事实而已。我一直觉得这名字更适合你。
t_en: Hmph, I was just telling the truth. I've always found this name to be more suitable for you.

t_ch: 所以为什么要来这儿，你在这当过园长？
t_en: So, why are we here? You managed this park before?

t_ch: 也可以这么形容。那时我还是一个孩子，为成为橡木家系的「铎音」，在歌斐木先生门下学习。
t_en: You could say that. I was still a child back then. To become the Bronze Melodia of the Oak Family, I'd gone under Mr. Gopher Wood's tutelage.

t_ch: 在一次平常的散步中，他临时起意，交予了我治理此地的权力。在他划定的方圆内，我的每一句话都将重如律法。
t_en: We were taking a walk in the park like any other day when he suddenly hit on the idea to have me manage the park. Within the domain he demarcated, my every word would hold the force of law.

t_ch: 现在想来，那或许是我第一次实践「秩序」。
t_en: In hindsight, that was probably the first time that I enforced Order.

t_ch: 你后来步步高升，看来是干得不赖咯？
t_en: You rose through the ranks soon after. I take it that you must have done a pretty good job?

t_ch: 恰恰相反，一片狼藉。所幸游客们只当那是一场狂欢，无人在意。
t_en: Quite the contrary — I made a terrible mess of things. Fortunately, the tourists thought of it as nothing more than a spell of merrymaking.

t_ch: 这算什么，历练一下你？
t_en: Was that his way of training you?

t_ch: 也许歌斐木先生是在以一种代价较小的方式，向我展现他的人生经验：规则不存在完美的范式。如果维系者不能维持自省，曾经高尚的决策也会日渐卑劣。
t_en: Perhaps he was just using an approach with lighter consequences to show me what he'd learned in life: That there is never an ideal paradigm when it comes to rules. If the enforcer is incapable of introspection, then even regulations once laid down with the noblest of intentions will eventually become defiled.

t_ch: 就像此时此地，被扭曲过后的「秩序」余音…只会徒增混乱。
t_en: This place is no exception. The distorted Afterechoes of the Order... will only add to the chaos.

t_ch: 「除汉堡外的食物均不可食用，尤其是冰淇淋」…该不会是你定的规矩吧？
t_en: "No consumption of food other than burgers, especially ice cream." ...Please tell me you didn't make this rule.

t_ch: 我对甜品并无偏见，当初立下的法则也只是叫人们不要哄抢，依序拿取。
t_en: I have nothing against desserts. The only reason I made that rule is so that people can learn to wait in line in an orderly manner for things instead of shoving and jostling people around.

t_ch: 违反了会怎么样？
t_en: What happens if I break the rule?

t_ch: 就像那位猎犬说的，会被纠正。
t_en: It's as the Bloodhound said: Corrective measures will be enforced.

t_ch: 我倒要试试看。
t_en: Hmph. Try me.

t_ch: 等等……
t_en: Wait, don't—

t_ch: 只一眨眼的工夫，面前的冰激凌消失了。
t_en: In the blink of an eye, the ice cream vanished.

t_ch: 这不什么都没发生吗？
t_en: Pfft. So, where are those corrective measures?

t_ch: 不应该是这样。
t_en: That's odd.

t_ch: 我说什么来着，轮不到你出手，家族自己就能解决问题。
t_en: Didn't I tell you? The Family can take care of the issue without you having to get involved.

t_ch: 「非演职人员请勿走上台前」——这条规矩倒是正常。
t_en: "Only cast and crew members are allowed on the stage." Hm, a surprisingly reasonable rule.

t_ch: 也容易验证，你我二人都谈不上「演职人员」。试试看吧。
t_en: And an easy one to test. Neither of us is part of the cast or a crew member. Let's try it out.

t_ch: 什么情况。
t_en: What's going on?

t_ch: 「行错路的，便引他们回到正途」——看来这就是猎犬口中的「纠正」了，和我当年的做法如出一辙。
t_en: "Guide those who have lost their way back onto the right path." Looks like this is an example of the "corrective measures" that the Bloodhound mentioned. It's exactly what I would've done back then.

t_ch: 合着你小时候就这么铁腕。
t_en: You've been iron-fisted since you were little, haven't you?

t_ch: …先等会儿。如果是以这种形式纠正，那我刚才吃掉的……
t_en: ...Wait a sec. If this is how violations are corrected, then everything that I ate just now...

t_ch: 我提醒过你的。
t_en: I tried to warn you.

t_ch: 如同路边常见的醉汉一般，他从口中吐出了一道彩虹——
t_en: Like a drunkard commonly seen by the sidewalk, he vomited a sliver of rainbow—

t_ch: 啊…啊……
t_en: Ugh...

t_ch: 感觉如何？
t_en: How do you feel?

t_ch: 我有点想杀了你……
t_en: Now, I'm feeling murderous...

t_ch: 总之，既然艾迪恩公园的麻烦与我有关，就不能置之不理。不需要筑梦师，我来根除这些扭曲。
t_en: In any case, say what you will, but I can't sit by and do nothing. Not when I'm the cause of all this trouble in Aideen Park. There's no need to involve the Dreamweavers. I'll fix these distortions once and for all.

t_ch: 你打算怎么做？
t_en: What's the plan?

t_ch: 并不复杂。任何扭曲都有根源，一旦显现便可追查。可以故意违背一些准则，只要我们能承担后果就行。
t_en: Nothing complicated. All distortions have their roots. We'll be able to trace them to the source once they manifest. Breaking a few rules will do. We just need to make sure that the corrective measures that will be implemented aren't too much for us to handle.

t_ch: 当浮现的余音试图将你我纠正时，我就能寻获它的所在。
t_en: When a distorted Afterecho manifests to correct us, I'll be able to pinpoint its whereabouts.

t_ch: 然后，就用「调律」抚平梦境吧。
t_en: Then I'll use the power of tuning to heal the Dreamscape.

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>「不可对鸟类态度恶劣。」</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Be kind to birds."</color>

t_ch: 看来也有些规则并未扭曲，这是我当时的原话。
t_en: Looks like some rules weren't distorted. That's a word-for-word account of what I said back then.

t_ch: 因为你妹妹叫「知更鸟」？
t_en: Did you make this rule because your sister is named Robin?

t_ch: 是因为歌斐木先生。他常常在公园里停留，观察众生百态。于先生而言，这也是疲惫时休憩的方式。
t_en: No, it was for Mr. Gopher Wood. He used to stop by the park to people-watch and appreciate the vibrancy of life. This was his way of recharging whenever things wore him out.

t_ch: 我希望他不要受到惊扰。
t_en: I wanted him to rest undisturbed.

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>「垃圾桶里禁止丢垃圾。」</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Do not discard trash in trashcans."</color>

t_ch: 太不讲道理了吧，这也是你订下的规矩？
t_en: This is absurd! Did you make this rule too?

t_ch: 我还是孩子时，也会对这种悖论感到好奇，想一探究竟。
t_en: Children tend to be fascinated by such paradoxes. I was no exception.

t_ch: 算了，我都不知道从什么角度笑话你。有什么适合丢进去的东西吗？
t_en: Forget it, I don't even know how to joke about this. Well, what do you suggest we throw in?

t_ch: 随便试试。
t_en: Anything will do.

t_ch: 哦，有反应了。
t_en: Oh, something's happening.

t_ch: 这是什么樵夫的垃圾桶吗？
t_en: What is this, a woodcutter's trashcan!?

t_ch: 暂时没有。
t_en: I don't have anything we can use for now.

t_ch: 你尝试投入了一些东西，但它完全没有任何反应——小垃圾桶真有个性，成功引起了你的注意力。
t_en: You attempt to insert some things, but no reaction is observed — This little trashcan has quite the personality, successfully grabbing your attention.

t_ch: 你将好看的垃圾放入了桶内以示重视，一转眼，桶中多出了更多好看的垃圾——真是聚宝盆原理的一种错误应用。
t_en: You insert Pleasant-Looking Trash into the bin as a sign of consideration, and in the blink of an eye, more Pleasant-Looking Trashes emerge — what an inappropriate application of the Horn of Plenty concept.

t_ch: 你放入了更多好看的垃圾，垃圾桶很高兴，向你默默致意，离开前，你莫名感到很有尊严。
t_en: You insert more Pleasant-Looking Trash. The trashcan is overjoyed and silently pays its respects to you. Before you leave, you suddenly feel a sense of dignity.

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>「体验游乐设施时，请勿使用惊梦剧团售卖的苏乐达瓶盖。」</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Please do not use SoulGlad bottle caps sold by the Dreamjolt Troupe while experiencing the attractions."</color>

t_ch: 哪有惊梦剧团，躲起来了？
t_en: The Dreamjolt Troupe? Where? Are they hiding or something?

t_ch: 嗷呜——
t_en: Awoooo!

t_ch: 在这呢，还挺会藏的。来吧，第一步是从它手里买瓶盖。
t_en: Oh, there it is! Found a good hiding spot, didn't ya? Come on, let's buy some bottle caps from it.

t_ch: 稍等。
t_en: Just one moment.

t_ch: 星期日将一些苜蓿币放在了气泡锂犬面前。
t_en: Sunday places some Alfalfa credits in front of Bubble Hound.

t_ch: 嗷嗷嗷呜——
t_en: Arf, arf, arroooo!

t_ch: 哎，我倒是听懂了，给你翻译翻译：「这点钱糊弄谁呢？」
t_en: Ahaha, let me translate that for you: "Who are you trying to pay with your spare change?"

t_ch: 星期日放上了更多的苜蓿币。
t_en: Sunday has placed even more Alfalfa credits.

t_ch: 如果这些还不够，就得劳烦你出手了。
t_en: I'm afraid you're going to have to handle this if it isn't enough.

t_ch: 出什么手？
t_en: Handle this? Handle what?

t_ch: …你不会就这点钱吧？
t_en: ...Wait, is that really all you've got on you!?

t_ch: 我现在的身份是逃犯。
t_en: May I remind you that I'm currently on the run?

t_ch: 嗷嗷——
t_en: Awoooo, awoooo!

t_ch: 惊梦剧团发出了短促的叫声，它向前凑了凑，将挂在身前的汽水瓶朝向两人。
t_en: The Dreamjolt Troupe lets out terse shouts. It moves closer, pointing the soda bottle hanging in front of it towards the two.

t_ch: 看来够了，运气还行。
t_en: Whew, luck's on our side. Looks like we scraped together just enough.

t_ch: 那我们试试看？就用这台扭蛋机。
t_en: Shall we give this capsule machine a try and see what happens?

t_ch: 开始游玩扭蛋机。
t_en: Play the capsule machine.

t_ch: 先等等。
t_en: Wait till later.

t_ch: 好像也没什么奇怪的。要不打开扭蛋看看。
t_en: Nothing's off so far. Maybe we should open the capsule?

t_ch: 呃，应该不会这么蛮不讲理吧？
t_en: Uh, they can't be seriously going that far, right?

t_ch: 喂喂喂…小心啊！
t_en: No, no, no! Hey, look out!

t_ch: 这根本不合理吧，它把扭蛋收回去不就行了？
t_en: Did they have to do that!? They could've just recalled the capsule!

t_ch: 也许让游乐设施不再是游乐设施，也是一种纠正的方式。
t_en: Perhaps making the attractions no longer just attractions is another corrective measure?

t_ch: 这规矩怎么还玩文字游戏的……
t_en: Ugh, aren't the rules being too finicky with words?

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>「请勿在花坛附近倒立前进。」</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Handstand walks around flowerbeds are prohibited."</color>

t_ch: 先不论猎犬是怎么发现这个事实的，感觉后果顶多也就是被翻回正面，要不你试试？我想看你倒立的样子。
t_en: Let's not worry about how the Bloodhounds figured this one out. Hm, I reckon the worst that can happen for this one is that they'll flip you back onto your feet. Say, why don't you test it out this time? I'd love to see you do a handstand!

t_ch: 容我拒绝。
t_en: No, thank you.

t_ch: 这就算了吧。都赖你，我再也不想吃冰淇淋了。
t_en: Uh, I say we pass on this. Thanks to you, I never want to eat ice cream again.

t_ch: 哦，出现了。该怎么说，和你还有几分像。
t_en: Oh, there it is. Huh, it does look somewhat like you.

t_ch: 让它就此消散吧。
t_en: Time for it to disappear for good.

t_ch: 「为我示现，一如往常——凡具瞳孔之物，皆有均等的魂灵。」
t_en: "Reveal thyself unto me, as is customary — All creatures endowed with eyes bear souls of equal worth."

t_ch: 还真有效果。公司该找个人盯着你的，就不怕你在匹诺康尼从头来过，再长出一双翅膀？
t_en: Whoa, it worked! The IPC totally should've sent someone to keep an eye on you. Aren't they worried that you might make a comeback in Penacony and grow a new pair of wings?

t_ch: 只要我不能抛头露面，就没有产生威胁的可能。
t_en: So long as there remains a need for me to keep a low profile, I'm no threat to them.

t_ch: 也对，你扮演的从来都是聚光灯下的角色，受人敬仰的精英、领袖。你上一次摔倒在泥地里是什么时候，是不是要追溯到童年了？
t_en: That's true. Come to think of it, you had always been in the spotlight, be it as a respected member of the elite or a leader. When was the last time you fell into the mud? All the way back during your childhood days?

t_ch: 是六岁前的事了。我像个普通男孩一样尝试用耳羽飞翔，却摔倒在土坑里，险些折断了天环。
t_en: Sometime before I turned six. Like most boys would, I tried to fly with my ear feathers, only to fall into a pit. I almost broke my halo.

t_ch: 我只想讽刺几句，你还真记得？
t_en: That was sarcasm, in case you didn't realize. But whoa, you actually remember something like that?

t_ch: 失败的瞬间总是难忘，人之常情。
t_en: You never forget times of failure. It's only human nature.

t_ch: 会把这种事当作失败，恐怕也独你一人了。
t_en: Bah, you're probably the only one who'd consider something like that a failure.

t_ch: 又怎么了？
t_en: What is it this time?

t_ch: 只是回望。
t_en: Just looking back.

t_ch: 那里什么都没有。
t_en: But nothing's there!

t_ch: 这正是故地重游的意义。在我眼中，一切都历历在目。
t_en: That's the whole point of revisiting a place. When I look at everything here, the memories are so fresh it's as if it all only happened yesterday.

t_ch: 年少的我在此处经历失败，为收拾残局疲于奔命。我本以为自己改变了许多，但现在看来仍是在原地打转。
t_en: When the younger me tasted failure here, I ran myself ragged to fix the mess. I'd thought myself no longer the same person as before... But it looks like I was just going round in circles.

t_ch: 很高兴能以这种形式重温过去。走吧，这场告别还很漫长。
t_en: I'm glad for this chance to look back on my past in this way. Come now, we should go. This will be a long farewell.

t_ch: 接下来去哪？
t_en: So, where are we heading next?

t_ch: 奥帝购物中心。不过那条路上也有猎犬，还是避开人流吧……
t_en: Oti Mall. There are Bloodhounds stationed along the way, though. It might be better to take a quieter route...

t_ch: 我无意冒犯，但二位最好不要接近那边。
t_en: I don't mean to meddle, but I'd advise you to stay away from that place.

t_ch: 怎么了这是？
t_en: Did something happen?

t_ch: 事态还不明朗，只是附近出现了一些异状，家族正在排查。
t_en: There isn't any concrete information yet, but anomalies have been reported around the area. The Family is currently investigating.

t_ch: …你还好么？
t_en: ...You okay?

t_ch: 我没事。
t_en: I'm fine.

t_ch: 虽然「星核」风波已经平息，但别有用心之人未必就不存在了。
t_en: The Stellaron incident may have been quelled, but who is to say that nobody has another agenda?

t_ch: 先生提醒得对，我们这就绕行。也请您多加小心。
t_en: Thanks for the tip, sir. We'll make a detour. May you stay safe, too.

t_ch: 请留步——
t_en: Hold on.

t_ch: 虽说美梦是安全的，但也请注意往来的车辆。
t_en: Sweet dreams are technically safe, but it would be wise to look out for oncoming vehicles.

t_ch: 多谢。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 那男的不是一般人啊，刚才没被发现真是奇迹。
t_en: Whew, that was no ordinary passerby. It's a miracle that he didn't see through your disguise.

t_ch: 星穹列车应该早就启程了，他为什么还在匹诺康尼？
t_en: The Astral Express should've departed a long time ago. Why is he still in Penacony...?

t_ch: …我们不必为此调整行程，但务必要多加留心。
t_en: ...We needn't change our plans because of him, but be sure to keep your guard up.

t_ch: 希望在我成为囚徒的这几天，匹诺康尼没有遇上新的麻烦。
t_en: I hope nothing terrible has fallen upon Penacony while I was in captivity the last few days.

t_ch: 是我心怀侥幸了。能允许我解释几句吗？
t_en: Seems I let my wishful thinking get the best of me. Will you at least give me a chance to explain?

t_ch: 可以，但在那之前——
t_en: I will. But before I do...

t_ch: 将双手放在背后，用短句回答我的问题。
t_en: Put your hands behind your back and answer my questions in short sentences.

t_ch: 短句？
t_en: Short sentences?

t_ch: 我得确保你的言语中没有暗含某种危险的吟诵。
t_en: I need to make sure your words aren't concealing any dangerous incantations.

t_ch: 我竟给各位留下了如此奇怪的印象么……
t_en: I can't believe I've left everyone with such a peculiar impression of me...

t_ch: 请相信，我并非带着恶意重返故地，「秩序」也不可能再临匹诺康尼了。
t_en: Please believe me when I say that I didn't come back here with ill intent. Penacony will never experience the Order again either.

t_ch: 在你使用过「同谐」的力量后，恐怕我很难轻信这番说辞。
t_en: I'm afraid I won't be taking your words at face value. Not after how you wielded the power of Harmony.

t_ch: 回答我的问题，你为何会出现在这里？
t_en: Answer me. What are you doing here?

t_ch: 要得到你的信任，想来我也只有一种选择。
t_en: It looks like there's only one way to gain your trust.

t_ch: 万维克，请出来吧。我们能仰赖的人多了一位。
t_en: Come on out, Wonweek. We've found ourselves an ally.

t_ch: 星期日向瓦尔特讲述了自己的经历，毫无保留……
t_en: Sunday unreservedly shares his story with Welt...

t_ch: 所以，是公司的人救了你？
t_en: So, it was the IPC that freed you?

t_ch: 应当是家妹与他们达成了某种约定。而我能在匹诺康尼行动自如，则多亏了这位万维克的帮助。
t_en: My sister has likely made an agreement of some sort with them. As for how I'm able to move around freely in Penacony, I have Wonweek to thank for that.

t_ch: 如您所见，我重返故乡只是为了和它道别。您是否愿意高抬贵手，给我一个不留遗憾的机会？
t_en: As you can see, I've only returned to my home to bid it farewell. Would you be kind enough to give me a chance to say goodbye so that I may leave without regrets?

t_ch: 保险起见，直到彻底离开匹诺康尼为止，你必须与我同行。
t_en: Agreed, but until you leave Penacony for good, you are to stay by my side.

t_ch: 感激不尽。
t_en: You have my gratitude.

t_ch: 我离开故乡时也同样匆忙，并非不能理解这种心情。
t_en: ...I left my home in a hurry, too... I know how it feels.

t_ch: 但这只是我的个人意见。对于此事，我的伙伴们同样有知情的必要。
t_en: That said, I can't make this call by myself. My companions need to know about this too.

t_ch: 星穹列车的其他成员也在匹诺康尼？
t_en: The Astral Express crew is in Penacony, too?

t_ch: 跟我去见他们吧。你也一起。
t_en: Come with me — we'll go find them. You come along too.

t_ch: 知道知道，这下热闹死了。
t_en: Okay, okay. Boy, this is gonna be a party!

t_ch: 我们到了。他们应该就在附近。
t_en: We're here. They should be somewhere nearby.

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生，我有一事感到不解。
t_en: Mr. Yang. If you don't mind, there's something that puzzles me.

t_ch: 盛会之星的风波已经平息，星穹列车本该再度踏上「开拓」之行。是什么让各位依旧在匹诺康尼停留？
t_en: Now that the crisis on the Planet of Festivities has been averted, shouldn't the Astral Express have departed to continue on its trailblazing journey? Why have you and your companions stayed behind in Penacony?

t_ch: 莫非在我之后，又有人掀起了风波？
t_en: Could it be that someone stirred up trouble after I left?

t_ch: 如果是，你会怎么做？
t_en: And if that were indeed the case?

t_ch: 我似乎也没有提议的立场。愿听从各位无名客的判断。
t_en: I doubt I'm in any position to be proposing ideas, but I'm happy to defer to the Nameless and aid with any plans you may have.

t_ch: 不用担心，匹诺康尼并无危险。我们仍未启程，只是因为一位特殊的「旅客」。
t_en: There's no need to worry, all is well in Penacony. Our reason for lingering here is a certain unusual passenger.

t_ch: 旅客？
t_en: A passenger?

t_ch: 列车受人所托，要送一位搭车客返回故乡，我们只是在匹诺康尼短暂歇脚……
t_en: The Express has been commissioned to send a passenger back home. We're just resting up in Penacony for a while before we depart.

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Hm?

t_ch: 您说的客人，莫非就是这位女士？
t_en: By "passenger," do you mean this lady here?

t_ch: 还是……
t_en: Or...

t_ch: 这些女士？
t_en: ...These ladies?

t_ch: 这，不应该……
t_en: This... How...

t_ch: 杨叔，你总算来了——大事不妙啦！
t_en: You're finally here, Mr. Yang! We have a huge problem!

t_ch: 可艾迪恩公园跟你有什么关系？创始人是你乔装打扮的？
t_en: But what has Aideen Park got to do with you? Don't tell me that the founder's one of your disguises?

t_ch: 我没有那么年长。
t_en: I'm not that old.

t_ch: 这只是个玩笑。
t_en: Oh, come on. Take a joke, will you?

t_ch: 来看看笔记上都写了什么……
t_en: Let's see what we have on these notes...

t_ch: 呃——「除汉堡外的食物均不可食用」？
t_en: "No consumption of food except for burgers." ...What?

t_ch: 下一条，去舞台边看看。
t_en: Next on the list is the stage. Let's go and check it out.
...Huh?

t_ch: 我们再试一次。
t_en: Let's try that again.

t_ch: 简单来说——现在，规则就是用来打破的？
t_en: So... Can I take it that rules are meant to be broken now?

t_ch: 似乎还不够，再去别处看看吧。
t_en: We're not breaking enough rules. We should look elsewhere too.

t_ch: 足够了，扭曲之处就在附近。
t_en: Enough. The location of the distortion is nearby.

t_ch: 动身吧。我们分开些走，保持几米距离。
t_en: Come now, it's time we set off. Let's not stick too close to each other, just in case. A few feet or so between us will do.

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 有机生命的历程总是充满变数，我常为此感到着迷。
t_en: Isn't it fascinating how variables never fail to find their way into the lives of organic lifeforms?

t_ch: 我也没想到会在匹诺康尼遇见螺丝咕姆先生，还以为天才俱乐部没有收到盛会的邀请函呢。
t_en: Neither did I expect to see you here in Penacony, Mr. Screwllum. I had assumed the Genius Society wasn't invited to the festivities.

t_ch: 我对「同谐」的庆典并无兴趣，谢绝邀请也是理所当然。但也因此错过了各位的精彩冒险，结论：追求合理也有坏处。
t_en: I have no interest in the Harmony's celebration, so it's only natural for me to turn down the invitation. But it looks like my decision also cost me the front-row seat to all of your exciting adventures. Conclusion: There is likewise a downside to pursuing reason.

t_ch: 如果各位天才莅临谐乐大典，故事的走向恐怕就截然不同了。
t_en: I'm sure if all of you geniuses showed up to the Charmony Festival, things would've been very much different.

t_ch: 听闻下一站，星穹列车与流光忆庭达成了合作？
t_en: Mm, I hear that the Astral Express has established a collaboration with the Garden of Recollection for your next destination?

t_ch: 他们只是提供了一个选项。是黑塔女士告诉您的吗？
t_en: They did suggest a destination, but that was all. Did you hear this from Madam Herta?

t_ch: 那毕竟是个无名客认知外的世界，所以我发信给她，希望包罗万象的「智识」能提供更多信息。
t_en: It's a world beyond the knowledge of the Nameless, after all. I wrote to her hoping that the all-encompassing Erudition could provide us with some information.

t_ch: 也许原因不仅如此。
t_en: Could there be more to it?

t_ch: 的确还有另一个原因：黑天鹅小姐提到翁法罗斯是个受三重命途影响的世界，而其中一条就是「智识」。
t_en: Yes, there is something else. Miss Black Swan said Amphoreus is a world influenced by three Paths, and the Erudition is one such Path.

t_ch: 逻辑：普通的命途行者不会在忆庭之镜留下痕迹。
t_en: Logic: The average Pathstrider wouldn't leave any trace behind in the Garden of Recollection's mirror.

t_ch: 不错。所以此事多半和令使，甚至和星神有关，我才好奇天才是否会有线索。
t_en: You're right, so this has to be related to the Emanators, or perhaps even the Aeons. That's why I wanted to know if the geniuses had any clue about it.

t_ch: 可惜俱乐部的会员往来甚少，大部分人也已死在寂静领主手上。我从未听说过「翁法罗斯」这个名字。
t_en: It's a pity we do not communicate much within the society, not to mention we've lost most of our members to the Lord of Silence. I have never heard of Amphoreus, however.

t_ch: 黑塔女士也一样。不过她倒是来了兴致，说要调查一番，大概是因为背后是流光忆庭吧，她一直对「记忆」的技术很感兴趣。
t_en: Madam Herta said the same, but it did pique her interest, and she agreed to do a little digging. My guess is that there's a chance the Garden of Recollection is behind it. She's always had a keen interest in the Remembrance's technology, after all.

t_ch: 这期间，列车也经她引荐结识了阮•梅女士，并为后者从「金伦加深域」带回了一具古兽遗骸。不过嘛……
t_en: It's also thanks to her that we got to make Miss Ruan Mei's acquaintance a while back. We even went into the Calenga Abyss to bring a Leviathan's carcass back for our new friend. Speaking of her...

t_ch: 谁也没想到，在阮•梅女士那儿，我们还会再遇见一位「故人」。
t_en: This is far too much an understatement, but none of us ever expected to see an old friend at Miss Ruan Mei's.

t_ch: 那位狐人少女的经历也堪称一段奇遇，也许这就是有机生命的缘分吧。
t_en: That foxian girl has indeed been through an unimaginable turn of events. I suppose that's the fate you organic lifeforms are bound by.

t_ch: 那可真是段不解之缘啊。
t_en: Yeah, a bond that truly runs deep.

t_ch: 所以星穹列车主动担起送她还乡的职责，是出于道义？
t_en: So, is it out of goodwill that the Astral Express volunteered to send her back home?

t_ch: 对无名客而言，正事与闲事真的存在一条界线么？
t_en: Would the Nameless see a point in keeping work and favors apart?

t_ch: 这也是她本人的请求，希望能在回仙舟前找个站点稍作停泊，整顿心思，毕竟对联盟来说…她的身份有些特别。
t_en: It's her request. She hopes to make a brief stop somewhere to reframe her mindset before returning to the Xianzhou... considering her unique position with the Alliance.

t_ch: 既然这位客人如此特殊，姬子小姐不用陪同吗？
t_en: Looks like this is one exceptional passenger. Don't you have to accompany her then, Miss Himeko?

t_ch: 交给我的同伴了，毕竟他们更了解「停云小姐」，旅游度假的事也是年轻人更在行。
t_en: My companions are with her. They know Miss Tingyun better anyway. Plus, those youngsters will definitely do a better job than me when it comes to vacations.

t_ch: 想必…他们玩得正开心吧？
t_en: They're probably... having a wonderful time now.

t_ch: 所以，有谁能为我解释一下吗？
t_en: So, is anyone going to tell me what's going on?

t_ch: 三月？
t_en: March?

t_ch: 啊，这个…那个…{NICKNAME}来吧！{M#他}{F#她}说话有条理。
t_en: Oh, uh... About that... Why don't you ask {NICKNAME} instead! {M#He}{F#She} knows how to present things logically!

t_ch: 显然，这里有许多停云。
t_en: Apparently, there are many Tingyuns here with us.

t_ch: 这点不用你强调啦，都看得出来！
t_en: Bah, you don't have to tell him that! He can see for himself!

t_ch: 我要打破你的刻板印象。
t_en: I'm gonna have to break that stereotype you have of me.

t_ch: 别挑这种场合啊！
t_en: Not now, of all times!

t_ch: 唉，还是我来吧…刚才分开后，我们就带着停云小姐四处逛逛，结果遇上了怪事。
t_en: Forget it. I'll tell you... We walked around with Miss Tingyun for a while after separating from the rest, but then we ran into something... weird.

t_ch: 是梦境出了什么问题？
t_en: Did something happen in the Dreamscape?

t_ch: 应该说是人祸…我们遇见一位皮皮西人，说自己在录不要笑挑战——请我们尝一种吃了就会大笑的糖。
t_en: Actually it's more like it was someone's fault... We met a Pepeshi filming a try-not-to-laugh challenge, and he gave us some of his candy. A type of candy that's supposed to make you fall over laughing.

t_ch: 合着是这种「不要笑挑战」啊。
t_en: Oh, so that sort of try-not-to-laugh challenge.

t_ch: 我说别瞎掺和，{M#他}{F#她}非要试试，结果试试就出事了呗。
t_en: I didn't really wanna do it, but {M#he}{F#she} insisted on us giving it a shot. That's how we got into this problem.

t_ch: 发生了什么？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 挑战大失败。
t_en: We failed the challenge. Terribly.

t_ch: 没吃饱。
t_en: I still wanted more candies.

t_ch: 狂笑三月七诞生！
t_en: Behold, the laughing maniac: March 7th!

t_ch: 哎呀——别打岔了。总之我俩吃了糖乐得不行，在地上滚了好几圈才站起来，起来就发现停云小姐……
t_en: Oh, come on—stop interrupting. So, we had some candy and were so happy we rolled around on the ground for a bit before standing up... But once we did, we realized Miss Tingyun...

t_ch: 停云小姐吃下糖，却开始不断打喷嚏！阿嚏！阿嚏——就像这样，然后她就在我们眼前稀里哐啷地变成了好几个停云！
t_en: Miss Tingyun ate the candy too, but she only sneezed non-stop! A-choo, a-choo! Sneeze after sneeze! And before we knew it, she multiplied into several Tingyuns right in front of us!

t_ch: …你有什么头绪？
t_en: ...What's your take on this?

t_ch: 梦境是忆质的世界，这种状况多半是她的大脑比较敏感，受到刺激后零散的记忆片段脱离，变成了个体。
t_en: The Dreamscape is a memoria world. It is likely the candy triggered her hypersensitive mind, causing scattered memory fragments to dissociate and become entities themselves.

t_ch: 不用担心，类似的事在美梦中不算少见。
t_en: There is no cause for concern. We've experienced similar incidents from time to time in the sweet dream.

t_ch: 杨叔，这位是？
t_en: Mr. Yang, who's this?

t_ch: 你可以卸下伪装了。
t_en: It's okay, you can reveal yourself now.

t_ch: 只是出于保险，否则这一路上，我们已经被猎犬拦下好几次了。
t_en: I was only being careful, or we would've been stopped by the Bloodhounds more than once on our way here.

t_ch: 万维克，为我揭下面具吧。
t_en: Help me remove my disguise, Wonweek.

t_ch: 许久不见，各位。
t_en: It's been a while, everyone.

t_ch: 你你你——什么情况？！
t_en: Y—You...! What's going on!?

t_ch: 什么情况？！
t_en: Yeah, what's going on!?

t_ch: 杨叔，你背叛了吗？
t_en: Mr. Yang! Have you turned on us!?

t_ch: 我还在做梦吗…
t_en: Am I still dreaming...?

t_ch: 稍安勿躁，这次换我来说明吧。
t_en: Everyone stay calm... I'll explain.

t_ch: 瓦尔特为两人讲述了此前的经历……
t_en: Welt fills them in on what he's been through...

t_ch: 事到如今，我认为星期日没有说谎的理由。为避免节外生枝，在他离开匹诺康尼前，我会一直同行。
t_en: I believe that at this point, Sunday has no reason to be dishonest. But to prevent any unforeseen complications, I'll be accompanying him everywhere he goes, until he leaves Penacony.

t_ch: 这位万维克先生则是星期日的旧识。
t_en: Mr. Wonweek here is an old acquaintance of Sunday's.

t_ch: 你好，美丽的女士。至于这位…更是重量级，就是你把这控制狂狠狠修理了一顿？
t_en: Pleased to meet you, fair lady. And you, of course... you're the heavyweight who pummeled this control freak?

t_ch: 不提当年勇。
t_en: I'm not one to boast about past feats.

t_ch: 他是一位好对手。
t_en: He was a worthy opponent.

t_ch: 都是大家的功劳。
t_en: Nah, it was a team effort.

t_ch: 今天可真是惊喜连连…上次是停云小姐，上上次是黄泉小姐，怎么感觉杨叔每次出门都会带些不得了的人回来啊？
t_en: The surprises just keep pouring in... First, there was Miss Acheron, and then Miss Tingyun. Why does it seem like Mr. Yang always comes back with some extraordinary guests?

t_ch: 咳咳，他的事先放一边，得赶紧让停云小姐恢复原状才行。
t_en: Ahem, let's leave this discussion for later. For now, we should be focusing on returning Miss Tingyun to her normal self.

t_ch: 这个…可能没那么简单。杨叔看看就知道了，这些停云小姐怕是有点难以沟通。
t_en: That's... not gonna be easy. You'll see what I mean, Mr. Yang. These Tingyuns aren't exactly easy to communicate with.

t_ch: 三月七小姐！此前的提议您考虑得如何了？
t_en: Miss March 7th! Have you taken the time to consider my suggestion?

t_ch: 提议？那个…您是哪一位停云小姐？
t_en: Your suggestion? Wait, which Miss Tingyun are you again...?

t_ch: 是我呀！准备与所有停云联手，成立「停云商团」的那位。
t_en: It's me! I'm the one who wants every Tingyun to come together to form the Tingyun Merchant Guild!

t_ch: 能与自己共事，机会实在难得，能有这般一心同体的默契，生意定可顺风顺水！
t_en: How often do you get the chance to work with yourself? Just imagine how in sync we'll be with one another... We're bound to thrive and prosper!

t_ch: 但我们没法离开梦境，做不了外贸，这不是见三月七小姐聪敏过人，想拉您来做对外接洽嘛！
t_en: I know we can't leave the Dreamscape to trade with the outside worlds, so that's where you come in, Miss March 7th! Your wits surpass many others, making you the perfect person to handle liaison negotiations on our behalf!

t_ch: 啊哈哈，我会考虑的…先祝你们一切顺利啦。
t_en: Ahaha, I guess I can consider it... I hope everything goes smoothly for your guild!

t_ch: 喏——杨叔，你看。
t_en: There. Now do you see what I mean, Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 确实有些自说自话。
t_en: There does appear to be an element of her talking to herself.

t_ch: 我也想变成好几个自己。
t_en: I want to clone copies of myself, too.

t_ch: 算了吧你，球棒都没法分。
t_en: Forget about it, how are you gonna clone your bat?

t_ch: 停云小姐也变幽默了。
t_en: Hah, Miss Tingyun's got a sense of humor these days.

t_ch: 你最好不是说「聪敏过人」那句。
t_en: Hey! You'd better not be referring to the part about my wits surpassing many others!

t_ch: 如此说来，瓦尔特先生曾提起过，列车此行是送一位旅客重返故土，这位女士的故乡可是仙舟联盟？
t_en: Mr. Yang mentioned the Express' next voyage is to send a passenger back home. Could this lady be from the Xianzhou Alliance?

t_ch: 正是仙舟「罗浮」。
t_en: Yes, more specifically the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 「罗浮」？那里前不久刚遭遇反物质军团的袭击，她有幸逃过一劫吗？
t_en: The Luofu? Didn't the Antimatter Legion launch an ambush on the Luofu some time ago? Was she one of the few survivors?

t_ch: 恰恰相反，她或许是距离风暴中心最近的人。
t_en: Quite the contrary. She might've been the person closest to the eye of the storm.

t_ch: 看来是段漫长的故事啊。
t_en: Looks like there's a lot more to this story.

t_ch: 先不提这个，身为梦境的东道主，你有什么办法吗？
t_en: A story for another time. As host of the Dreamscape, do you have any suggestions for fixing this problem?

t_ch: 一时还难以判断，再和附近的停云女士谈谈吧。
t_en: I don't have much to go on now. Perhaps we should talk to the other Miss Tingyuns here.

t_ch: 几位又来啦？考虑得如何？
t_en: Oh, you're here again! So, what's your decision?

t_ch: 呃…还在琢磨。我太抢手了，这种事得慢慢考虑。
t_en: Uhh... I'm still deliberating. I—I'm so highly sought after that it's only right I give it careful consideration.

t_ch: 哎呀——我眼光果然从不出错。您慢慢考虑，倒也不急。
t_en: Aha—! I knew it, I always have an eye for talented people. No problem, take your time.

t_ch: 不错，亲自烹上一席好菜，总能使人念念不忘。
t_en: That sounds nice. People never forget a personally prepared home-cooked meal.

t_ch: 但既然是赠礼，总要讲究个细水长流，案上粲花连日不重，不是更能给人添上好兴致？
t_en: But then again, gifts should be lasting. What about a delightful new bouquet of flowers on the table each day? Wouldn't they do a better job at brightening up someone's day?

t_ch: 嗯，这位说的很有道理。
t_en: Hmm, that sounds like a good idea.

t_ch: 可真要细究，心思是最不可忽视的，越是繁复，越能让人感到情谊呢。
t_en: If we really want to do this right though, it's the thought behind the gift that truly matters. The more thought goes into the gift, the better the recipient feels it.

t_ch: 哎呀，这位的观点也相当有见地。
t_en: Oh, she makes a valid point, too.

t_ch: 你还真是谁都不得罪……
t_en: You sure have a way of not offending anyone...

t_ch: 看来这三位停云小姐还要争论一会儿，咱们插不上话。
t_en: These three Tingyun's don't look like they'll be reaching a conclusion anytime soon. We won't be able to get a word in.

t_ch: 老日，你怎么看？
t_en: So, what do you think, Sunny?

t_ch: 还是换种称呼吧，朋友。
t_en: Let's try a different name, my dear friend.

t_ch: 显得亲切点呗，就跟这位狐人小姐的习惯一样。
t_en: Don't we sound chummier that way? I took a leaf out of this foxian girl's book.

t_ch: 停云女士的麻烦，和我此前的猜测一样。在经受了那场作弄后，她零落成了自身的每一个音符。
t_en: The problem with Miss Tingyun is just as I suspected before. That prank shattered her into notes that are each a part of her.

t_ch: 换言之，散落在这里的是「停云」的一道道面相。
t_en: In other words, it means the Tingyuns we see here are all different aspects of her psyche.

t_ch: 感觉你的解释反而把事情变复杂了哎……
t_en: Okaaay, you just made it sound a whole lot more complicated...

t_ch: 总之，在梦中这并非个例，使用「调律」或许能解决各位的困扰。
t_en: At any rate, this is a known occurrence across the Dreamscape. Tuning could be the way to fix it.

t_ch: 只是，这些停云女士都太我行我素了些，有哪位是能够沟通的吗？
t_en: The only problem is that these Miss Tingyuns are too caught up in their own world. Is there one we can properly speak with?

t_ch: 能够沟通……啊，好像是有。
t_en: One that we can speak with...? Oh, I think there is one!

t_ch: 呃，不过我得试试，还能不能认出来。
t_en: But... It may take me a while to identify her.

t_ch: 好像还是没商量出什么来啊……
t_en: Tsk, looks like they still haven't decided on anything yet...

t_ch: 要我说，把那位打圆场的带走，很快就能看到结果了。
t_en: Well, if you ask me... Take the people pleaser out of the picture, and they'll arrive at a conclusion in no time at all.

t_ch: 你是想看她们打起来吧……
t_en: You're hoping for them to get into a fight, aren't you?

t_ch: 唔…啧…呼……这倒真是怪梦啊…梦着自己在大街上走了……
t_en: Nngh... Tsk... Phew... I'm... walking on the street...? What a weird dream...

t_ch: 这位停云小姐好像在梦游，应该不行。
t_en: This Miss Tingyun looks like she's sleepwalking. Nope, not her.

t_ch: 好吓人的气势！和她聊不来……
t_en: Yikes, she's scary! No, I won't be able to talk to her...

t_ch: 哦？呵呵呵……
t_en: Ooh? Heh heh heh...

t_ch: 她似乎完全无法感知到其他事，只是站在那里，不断地操作着喷钞机，偶尔发出兴奋的笑声。
t_en: She seems completely oblivious to everything else, and is just standing there, continuously operating the Cash-Spewer, occasionally letting out an excited laugh.

t_ch: 总觉得打扰她会很危险……
t_en: Why do I feel like it's not gonna be a wise move to interrupt her...?

t_ch: 你…怎么不会飞呀？
t_en: You... can't fly? Why?

t_ch: 我就会哦？很久以前，有一个大姐姐…就带我飞到过很高很高的地方呢。
t_en: I can, I can fly! A long, long time ago, a big girl flew with me really high into the sky!

t_ch: 下一次，也带上你一起去，好不好？
t_en: I'll take you next time. Pinky promise?

t_ch: 呃…是不是该找个人陪着她？
t_en: Uh... Should we get someone to watch over her?

t_ch: 我反倒觉得她很安全——成年人心中的童真，往往都坚硬得很！
t_en: Nah, I think she's perfectly fine on her own. The childlike innocence that adults have is a lot more resilient than you can imagine!

t_ch: 就算你说得有道理，也不能套用在这里吧。
t_en: Even if that's true, it doesn't apply to her!

t_ch: 呀，几位莫不是来找小女子的？
t_en: Oh, were you all looking for me?

t_ch: 找到啦！如果我没记错，这位停云小姐的特点是……
t_en: Found her! If I remember correctly, this Miss Tingyun's trait is...

t_ch: 只是些措辞上的习惯。
t_en: It's nothing special really.

t_ch: 就是她了。
t_en: There she is.

t_ch: 这也是停云小姐的一个侧面。
t_en: So this is also a side of Miss Tingyun.

t_ch: 这应该符合你口中「能够沟通」的标准吧？
t_en: She ought to meet your "one we can properly speak with" requirement, right?

t_ch: 可以一试。
t_en: Worth a try.

t_ch: 这位女士，我希望能对您进行「调律」。
t_en: My lady, if I may, I wish to perform a tuning on you.

t_ch: 事先说好，咱们可都看着你呢，如果想拿停云小姐当人质——
t_en: Before you start, you should know we're keeping our eyes on you. If you even think about taking Miss Tingyun hostage—

t_ch: 得请你做好被引力撕裂的准备了。
t_en: Brace yourself for gravitational disintegration.

t_ch: 这里三人可长着四对眼睛。
t_en: Know that there are four pairs of eyes between the three of us.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼最不容小觑的都在这了。
t_en: Remember, we're the most formidable people in all of Penacony.

t_ch: 我无意挑战各位。保险起见，由我这位朋友代劳吧——就「同谐」而言，万维克的造诣比我更为精深。
t_en: It is not my intention to provoke you. Perhaps as assurance, we can have my friend here perform the task in my stead. Wonweek has a higher mastery of the Harmony than I do.

t_ch: 这倒是实话。
t_en: That is true.

t_ch: 若再不放心，在他进行「调律」时，各位也尽可对我加以控制。
t_en: If you still have concerns, you are also free to restrain me while he performs the tuning.

t_ch: 呃，这倒也不必…你这么坦诚，反倒给我整不会了。
t_en: Um, there's no need for that. You're acting so sincere. I'm actually surprised.

t_ch: 停云小姐没问题吧？
t_en: Are you alright with this, Miss Tingyun?

t_ch: 小女子随波逐流，哪有什么意见呢，列位决定便好。
t_en: I go as the wind blows, so you may decide for me.

t_ch: 失礼了。万维克，请尽你所能吧。
t_en: Then kindly forgive our intrusion. Please do your best, Wonweek.
Ah...

t_ch: 呵呵……
t_en: Hee-hee...

t_ch: 什么鬼？
t_en: What the...

t_ch: 刚才那声音，你们听到了吗？
t_en: Did any of you hear a voice just now?

t_ch: 声音？
t_en: A voice?

t_ch: …没什么。
t_en: Never mind...

t_ch: 啊，看——起效果了！
t_en: Ah, look! It's working!

t_ch: 停云小姐，你可算回来了！
t_en: Miss Tingyun, you're finally back!

t_ch: 看你个子小小的，人倒是挺厉害嘛……
t_en: You may be small, but you're pretty amazing!

t_ch: 咦，你怎么啦？
t_en: Huh? What's wrong?
Oof...

t_ch: 没什么，小事一桩……
t_en: It's nothing. Just a trivial matter...

t_ch: 你感应到了什么？
t_en: What do you sense?

t_ch: 真该让你自己来的。这姑娘根本没看起来那么简单……
t_en: I should have let you handle it yourself. This girl is not as ordinary as she appears...

t_ch: 我刚才…差点就死了一次……
t_en: I nearly died... just now...

t_ch: 黑暗在眼前沉降，如同宇宙诞生前的混沌。在一片混沌中，一个声音像造物者那般响起……
t_en: Darkness descends before the eyes, reminiscent of the chaos before the birth of the universe. Amidst this chaos, a voice emerged, resonating like that of the creator...

t_ch: 「别害怕。」
t_en: "Don't be afraid."

t_ch: 「无论看见什么，你只需记得一件事。」
t_en: "No matter what you see, you only need to remember one thing."

t_ch: 「你并非孤身一人。」
t_en: "You're not alone."

t_ch: 这是…什么地方？
t_en: What... is this place?

t_ch: 你…是谁？
t_en: Who... are you?

t_ch: 我…又是谁？
t_en: And... who am I?

t_ch: 那里…是我的来处吗？
t_en: Is that... where I came from?

t_ch: 这条路又通向何方？
t_en: And where does this path lead?

t_ch: 维生舱搞定了，都是小问题。
t_en: The life support pod is ready. There were only minor issues.

t_ch: 太好了。不然，那仙舟人怕是要撑不住了。
t_en: That's great. Otherwise, that person from the Xianzhou might not be able to hold on much longer.

t_ch: 你们还在尝试？根本没有成功的先例，被绝灭大君「毁灭」的人真的还有救吗？
t_en: You're still trying? There's no precedent for saving someone who was destroyed by a Lord Ravager. Will it even work?

t_ch: 我不是她，不理解背后的逻辑，但那位天才说可以…就一定能做到。
t_en: I'm not a genius, so I don't understand the logic behind it, but if she says it's possible, then it must be.

t_ch: 两位，请问这是何处？
t_en: Excuse me, could you tell me where I am?

t_ch: …他们听不见我说话吗？
t_en: ...Can they not hear me?

t_ch: 这位姑娘是……
t_en: This girl is...

t_ch: 「停云」…好熟悉的名字，是我认识的人吗？
t_en: "Tingyun"... What a familiar name. Do I know her?

t_ch: 这些是……
t_en: These are...

t_ch: 「这是『毁灭』留下的伤口，已经在你体内生根，挥之不去。」
t_en: "These are wounds left by the Destruction. They have taken root within your body and cannot be removed."

t_ch: 「毁灭」？我不太明白……
t_en: The Destruction? I don't understand...

t_ch: 请问您是谁？为何一直不愿现身？
t_en: Please, who are you? And why won't you show yourself?

t_ch: 「我无法来到你的面前，能做的只有等待你走完这段路。」
t_en: "I can't go to you. All I can do is wait for you to finish walking this path."

t_ch: 您是要我通过这里？我…要和它对抗吗？
t_en: You want me to go through here? Do I have to... fight it?

t_ch: 「没有必要，那太危险，你也做不到。」
t_en: "There's no need for that. It's too dangerous, and you can't do it anyway."

t_ch: 「不用抗争，去接纳它，试着和它共处，就像抚摸自己的疤痕。」
t_en: "Don't fight it, embrace it. Try to coexist with it. Think of it as feeling your own scars."

t_ch: 疤痕？
t_en: My scars?

t_ch: …等等？！
t_en: ...Wait!?

t_ch: 怎么会…为何还有这么多？
t_en: How can it be... Why are there still so many of them?

t_ch: 方才的，也未能消灭吗？
t_en: The ones from before weren't defeated either?

t_ch: 「我说过你消灭不了它们，没有人能做到。」
t_en: "As I said before, you can't defeat them. No one can."

t_ch: 「要么与『毁灭』的伤痕一同死去，坠入黑暗。」
t_en: "You can fall into the darkness and die alongside these scars of the Destruction."

t_ch: 「要么到这边来，重新回到世上，带着它们一起。这是二选一。」
t_en: "Or bring them with you when you return to the world here. These are your two choices."

t_ch: 重新回到世上？您在说什么……
t_en: Return to the world? What are you saying...?

t_ch: 啊…难怪那两个人对我视而不见。
t_en: Aah... No wonder those two ignored me.

t_ch: 我…已沦作孤魂野鬼了么？
t_en: Am I just a wandering spirit now?

t_ch: 「很接近，但还不是。」
t_en: "Close, but not quite."

t_ch: 那就是徘徊在生死边缘，犹如中阴之身？
t_en: So, I'm trapped between life and death, existing in a limbo?

t_ch: 「可以这么理解。」
t_en: "You can think of it that way."

t_ch: …照您的意思，即便我能够醒来，这些置我于死地的创伤，也会存留此身，永不愈合？
t_en: ...Based on what you said, even if I should wake up, these wounds that caused my death will remain on my body and never heal?

t_ch: 小女子不明白，这位恩人似乎是想救我，可为何又要告知这些？
t_en: I don't understand. You appear to be trying to save me, benefactor, but why are you telling me all this?

t_ch: 「算是我的习惯。对于是否要来到这个世界，我想每个人都有选择的权利。」
t_en: "It's a habit of mine. I believe everyone should have the right to choose whether they wish to return to this world or not."

t_ch: 就算您这么说，小女子也想不起任何事。似乎没什么能支持我作出决定。
t_en: Be that as it may, I don't remember anything. There's nothing to help me make a choice.

t_ch: 「无妨。我相信对『生命』的渴望，本就诞生于一无所有中。」
t_en: "That doesn't matter. I believe the desire to live was born from nothing to begin with."

t_ch: 「你也可以证明自己是个例外。那同样是我乐见的结果。」
t_en: "You may also prove yourself to be the exception. I would also be pleased with that result."

t_ch: …麻烦啊，活着总是如此。
t_en: ...How troublesome, but I suppose that's part of living.

t_ch: 「生存和毁灭，于你而言不再是选择，它们生长在同一条路上。」
t_en: "Survival or Destruction is no longer a choice for you. They both now grow along the same path."

t_ch: 「而经由这条道路——」
t_en: "And only by walking this path—"

t_ch: 「你才能够穿破黑暗，来到我的身边。」
t_en: "Will you break through the darkness and arrive at my side."

t_ch: 清醒一点了吗？
t_en: Feeling more awake?

t_ch: 恭喜你。在一场拉锯中获得了最初的胜利，这是个好的开始。
t_en: Congratulations. You attained your very first victory in this tug of war. It's a good start.

t_ch: 我…这是……
t_en: I... This is...

t_ch: 简单来说吧。
t_en: Let me explain.

t_ch: 你遭受了绝灭大君的袭击，依照常理，绝不可能活下来。
t_en: You were attacked by a Lord Ravager. By all rights, you should not have survived.

t_ch: 但有人不这么认为，而我恰好能满足他的要求。
t_en: But someone didn't agree with that assessment, and it just so happens I was able to fulfill his request.

t_ch: 商队的其他人呢……
t_en: What about the other merchants?

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 为什么是我……
t_en: Why me...?

t_ch: 因为你最为不幸，偏偏落入{RUBY_B#幻胧}那位大君{RUBY_E#}手中，肉体和精神都遭受了毁灭。
t_en: Because you were the most unfortunate. You fell into the hands of {RUBY_B#Phantylia}that Lord Ravager{RUBY_E#} who destroyed your body and spirit.

t_ch: 但你又是幸运的，她要将你的一切据为己有，这需要时间，你才免于在一瞬间灰飞烟灭。
t_en: Yet you were also the most fortunate. Her desire to claim everything about you for herself meant she needed time, so you were spared instant annihilation.

t_ch: 「死而复生」和「死里逃生」，二者只有一线之隔，却天差地别。如果你彻底死亡，我也无能为力。
t_en: There is only a fine line between resurrection and escaping death, yet they couldn't be more different. If you had truly died, I would have been powerless to do anything for you.

t_ch: 身体…好怪……
t_en: My body... feels strange...

t_ch: 抱歉，现在还不能让你恢复知觉。你会因此痛不欲生。
t_en: I'm sorry. I can't let you regain your ability to feel just yet. You'd be in excruciating pain.

t_ch: 这副身躯被「毁灭」浸染，遭受了最严重的破坏，我说过，这种创伤会一直跟随着你。
t_en: Your body was contaminated by the Destruction and suffered severe damage. As I said before, these wounds will stay with you for the rest of your life.

t_ch: 为避免死灰复燃，我对你的身体——准确地说，尾巴——进行了一些微小的优化。
t_en: To prevent its resurgence, I made some minor modifications to your body — or more specifically, your tail.

t_ch: 学会适应它吧。幸存者的代价往往是「不幸」，这也无可奈何。
t_en: You'll have to learn to adapt to it. Unfortunately, the inescapable price of survival is often more adversity.

t_ch: …无论如何，多谢您的救命之恩。
t_en: ...Regardless, I'm grateful to you for saving my life.

t_ch: 不必道谢，这只是一场交易。
t_en: There's no need for that. This was merely a transaction.
